rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HULA MAE MCCLENDON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/10/2026. Issue necessitating continuance: notice to Inheritance Advanced is insufficient. Notice was mailed on January 20, 2026, just fourteen days prior to the hearing. At least 15 days before the time set for the hearing, the petitioners shall cause notice of the time and place of the hearing to be delivered to the person required to be given notice (Probate Code Section 1215(a)(1)). Please address the above issue and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 02/24/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE COLIN M. KING AND JEANNIE M. KING REVOCABLE LI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Establishing Revocable Living Trust And Confirming Trust Assets Probate Code 850 Et Seq
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review additional papers that have been submitted since the last hearing and determine whether the statute of frauds under Probate Code section 15206 is satisfied to validate a trust in relation to real property. Other than the decedent's 2021 hadnwritten note attached to the supplement previously filed, the only written instrument signed by the decedent is his e-mail sent to his prior estate planning attorney Michael Woods in 2021. While the e-mail may qualify as a written instrument signed by the decedent and contemplated by the statute of frauds, it appears to show the decedent's mere desire to "discuss" with his attorney "Prop 19 and the transfer of property" after the decedent completed an estate planning questionnaire, rather than the decedent's declaration contemplated by section 15200(a) that he has transferred his real property to himself as trustee (or presently holds such real property as trustee). The decedent's verbal statements made in 2025 to his prior estate planning attorney and his nurse about his plans to finalize the trust and transfer his property to the trust, as described in the additional papers, do not appear to satisfy the statute of frauds. See also Osswald v. Anderson (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 812, 818 ["Although Ellis's testimony related the Osswalds' intent that their home was to be the trust property, the statute of frauds forbids the creation of an express trust in real property by verbal declaration of the owner."] PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENIS ALBERTO MENDOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/24/2026. The following issues were e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 01/16/2026: 1) No proposed order (DE-140 form) has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form for confidential filing. 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) A verified supplement must be filed in response to the following: i) Item 2d of petition: At least one box must be checked and completed. ii) Item 3d must be completed. iii) Item 5a(7) or (8): At least one box must be checked. iv) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. v) Item 8: The addresses of Matilde Mendoza and Denis Mendoza must be stated. If they are minors, their age must be stated.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH LEONARD KNOX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/03/2026 to be considered with competing petition. The following issues must be addressed prior to the next hearing: 1) Petition is incomplete and/or inconsistent: a) the caption does not indicate authorization to administer under IAEA, but Item 2c requests full authority under IAEA. Please clarify. b) Item 3e(3) indicates that all heirs at law have waived bond, but Patricia Brown has not waived bond. c) Item 6f is marked but it appears Item 6b applies. d) Item 8 must include the name and relationship to decedent. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. In no event will the bond be less than $20,000. 3) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 4) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A).
